revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=御杖神社 |画像=[[画像:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]神末1020 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 39 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 11 | 経度秒 = 40 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = left |祭神=[[岐神|久那斗神]]
八街比古神
八街比女神 |本殿=[[神明造]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=（伝）[[垂仁天皇]]時代 |例祭=[[12月5日]] |地図= Japan Nara }} '''御杖神社'''（みつえじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[岐神|久那斗神]] *八街比古神 *八街比女神 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によると、第11代[[垂仁天皇]]の勅命により、[[倭姫命]]は[[大和国]]笠縫邑より御杖代として[[天照大御神]]を祀るための土地を求めて各地を巡ったが、[[伊勢国]]（今の[[伊勢神宮]]）に向かう際、この地に行宮を造り御休座になったことが創祀とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]（[[927年]]）では[[大和国]][[宇陀郡]]17座の一座として記されている。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）争乱のため荒廃したが、同23年（[[1554年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]と定められた。 == 現地情報 == [[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]神末1020 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[元伊勢]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みつえしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == Location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == Related Topics == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[ja:御杖神社]]

Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == Related Topics == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:御杖神社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == Related Topics == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mitsu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Mitsu {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == Related Topics == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsu Shrine-qq]] It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == Related Topics == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsu Shrine-qq]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mitsu Shrine-qq]] Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}).

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Mitsu Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}).

{{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mitsu {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] Haiden | location = [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue]], [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara }} '''Mitsue Shrine''' ({{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社}}, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mitsu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Mitsue Shrine]] [[sn:Mitsu Shrine]] [[ja:御杖神社]]

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:御杖神社 up to rev 84393427
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=御杖神社 |画像=[[画像:Mitsue jinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]神末1020 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 39 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 11 | 経度秒 = 40 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |ラベル位置 = left |祭神=[[岐神|久那斗神]]
八街比古神
八街比女神 |本殿=[[神明造]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=（伝）[[垂仁天皇]]時代 |例祭=[[12月5日]] |地図= Japan Nara }} '''御杖神社'''（みつえじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == *[[岐神|久那斗神]] *八街比古神 *八街比女神 == 歴史 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝によると、第11代[[垂仁天皇]]の勅命により、[[倭姫命]]は[[大和国]]笠縫邑より御杖代として[[天照大御神]]を祀るための土地を求めて各地を巡ったが、[[伊勢国]]（今の[[伊勢神宮]]）に向かう際、この地に行宮を造り御休座になったことが創祀とされる。 [[延喜式神名帳]]では[[大和国]][[宇陀郡]]17座の一座として記されている。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]15年（[[1546年]]）争乱のため荒廃したが、同23年（[[1554年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に[[郷社]]と定められた。 == 境内社 == *木花佐久夜毘女神社（[[木花開耶姫命]]） *龍田神社（[[志那都比古神]]・志那都比女神） *藪中神社（[[久延毘古|久延毘古命]]） *愛宕神社（[[迦具土命]]） *秋葉神社（迦具土命） *金刀毘羅神社（[[大物主命]]） *稲荷神社（[[大田命]]） == 現地情報 == [[奈良県]][[宇陀郡]][[御杖村]]神末1020 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[元伊勢]] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みつえしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:御杖村]]

According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[Emperor Suinin]], [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mitsu Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Motoise]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] }} [[sn:Mitsue Shrine]] [[sn:Mitsu Shrine]] [[ja:御杖神社]]

It is listed as a [[Shikinaisha]] (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. When passing through this area on her way to [[Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[sn:Mitsue Shrine]] [[sn:Mitsu Shrine]] [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}}

It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[sn:Mitsue Shrine]] [[sn:Mitsu Shrine]] [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|qid=Q930076}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Mitsue Shrine[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[sn:Mitsue Shrine]] [[sn:Mitsu Shrine]] [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|WD=Q930076}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|WD=Q930076}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|WD=Q930076}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Mitsue, Nara|Mitsue Village]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:御杖神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q54153270}}

It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ([[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, [[:en:Uda District, Nara|Uda District]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御杖神社]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ({{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}}) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御杖神社]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mitsue Shrine]][[ja:御杖神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Mitsue Shrine]][[ja:御杖神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mitsue Shrine]][[ja:御杖神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Mitsue Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mitsue jinja.JPG | location = {{ill|Mitsue, Nara|en|Mitsue, Nara|WD=Q930076|lt=Mitsue}}, {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | latitude = 34.4942 | longitude = 136.1944 | label_position = left | deity = [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]]
Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami
Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami | main_building_style = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | classification = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine)
Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|WD=Q930076}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mitsue, Nara|en|Mitsue, Nara|WD=Q930076|lt=Mitsue Village}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ({{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}}) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御杖神社]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ({{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}}) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御杖神社]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Former [[Gōsha]] (Village Shrine) | founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} | annual_festival = [[December 5]] | map_type = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Mitsue|WD=Q930076|1=Mitsue, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.494167|N|136.194444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Mitsue Shrine|ja|御杖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsue Shrine|qq=}} (御杖神社, ''Mitsue Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Mitsue, Nara|en|Mitsue, Nara|WD=Q930076|lt=Mitsue Village|1=Mitsue, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and held the status of a Gōsha (Village Shrine) under the former shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * [[Kunitsu Kami|Kunado-no-Kami]] * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Kami * Yachimata-Hime-no-Kami == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to tradition, during the reign of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] journeyed across the land seeking a suitable place to enshrine [[Amaterasu]]. When passing through this area on her way to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, she built a temporary palace (''gyokū'') here and rested, which is said to mark the shrine's origin. The shrine is listed as one of the 17 shrines in [[Uda District, Yamato Province|Uda District]] in the [[Engishiki]] (a Heian-period book of laws and regulations). In 1546 (15th year of the [[Tenbun era]]), the shrine fell into disrepair due to conflict but was rebuilt in 1554 (23rd year of Tenbun). It was designated a Gōsha (Village Shrine) in 1873. == Sub-Shrines == The following sub-shrines are located within the shrine grounds: * Konohanasakuya Shrine ([[Konohanasakuya-hime]]) * Tatsuta Shrine ([[Shinatsuhiko-no-Kami|Shinatsuhiko]] and Shinatsuhime) * Yabunaka Shrine ({{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko|1=Kuebiko}}) * Atago Shrine ([[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|Kagutsuchi]]) * Akiba Shrine (Kagutsuchi) * Kotohira Shrine ([[Ōmononushi-no-Kami|Ōmononushi]]) * Inari Shrine ([[Ōta-no-Mikoto|Ōta-no-Kami]]) == location == 1020 Kamisuye, Mitsue, {{ill|Uda District, Nara|en|Uda District, Nara|WD=Q1131315|lt=Uda District|1=Uda District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan == See Also == * [[List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] * [[Motoise]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|御杖神社|version=84393427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御杖神社]] {{Commons category|Mitsue Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q54153270}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mitsue, Nara]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
